preview_text:
The model favors Portugal marginally, assigning a 35% win probability against Colombia's 33%. The gap between the two is narrow—less than 2 percentage points—suggesting the teams are nearly evenly matched in the statistical forecast.

The distribution across outcomes indicates a competitive match. With draw probability at 32%, a stalemate is plausible and only modestly less likely than either team's outright victory. The win probabilities are closely bunched, meaning the model sees little daylight between the three outcomes; no scenario dominates decisively.

The market consensus diverges from the model. Sportsbooks assign Portugal a 45% win probability—roughly 10 percentage points higher than the model's estimate—while compressing Colombia to 27% and draws to 29%. Polymarket prices move even further in Portugal's direction, pushing the win probability to 47%. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source better reflects the true match outcome; the divergence is meaningful and worth noting.
